name: Physical
description: Physics-based particle simulation with wind and gravity forces

stateSize._role: Sizing-only (state texture edge). Inherited from pointsEmit; NOT read by the shader (the agent shader derives state dims from the bound state texture). Used by resolveDimension via the {param:'stateSize'} state-texture specs below.

feedback._comment: Agent-based middleware in a particle pipeline (pointsEmit -> physical -> pointsRender). Physical does NOT create, deposit, or scatter agents -- it has NO drawMode 'points' pass, NO diffuse pass, NO trail texture. State lives in PERSISTENT 'global_'-prefixed textures created by pointsEmit and SHARED across the pipeline (definition.js textures:{} is EMPTY -- physical does not own them). The runtime scopes them per particle pipeline (global_xyz_<pipelineId>, etc.) and treats them as state surfaces: isStateSurface matches the _xyz/_vel/_rgba suffix, so they persist frame-to-frame WITHOUT an end-of-frame swap (ref 04 §10.7); within-frame writes ping-pong (§10.2). The agent pass is MRT (drawBuffers:3) writing all three in one pass: global_xyz=[x,y,z,alive], global_vel=[vx,vy,vz,seed], global_rgba=[r,g,b,a]. Out-of-bounds agents are flagged alive=0 (pointsEmit respawns them next frame). State format MUST be rgba32f (full float) per reference. No drawMode/count/countUniform/blend/repeat anywhere in this effect. The agent pass renders into the stateSize texture but the agent body never reads `resolution` (the WGSL Uniforms.resolution member is unused), so no resolution hazard.
